rivaroxaban.notes: Avoid if CrCl <15. P-gp and CYP3A4 inhibitors increase exposure.

amlodipine.mechanism: Blocks L-type calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle → vasodilation
amlodipine.food_effect: Grapefruit juice inhibits intestinal CYP3A4 → Cmax +35%, AUC +40%. Avoid entirely.
amlodipine.notes: Long half-life → once daily dosing. Minimal negative inotropy vs non-DHP CCBs.

lisinopril.food_effect: Potassium-rich foods (bananas, oranges) may increase hyperkalemia risk
lisinopril.notes: Reduce dose in CrCl <30. Contraindicated in pregnancy (teratogenic). ACE cough in 10-15%.

enalapril.notes: Requires renal activation. Monitor K+ especially with K-sparing diuretics.

losartan.notes: Active metabolite EXP3174 is more potent. Less cough than ACEi.

atenolol.notes: Hydrophilic — renal excretion, reduce dose in renal impairment. Less CNS effects than propranolol.

digoxin.mechanism: Inhibits Na+/K+-ATPase → increased intracellular Ca++ → positive inotropy
digoxin.food_effect: High-fiber meal reduces absorption 25%. Take consistently relative to meals.
digoxin.notes: P-gp substrate — many interactions. Hypokalemia potentiates toxicity. Signs: nausea, yellow-green halos.

furosemide.mechanism: Inhibits Na+/K+/2Cl- cotransporter in thick ascending loop of Henle

furosemide.notes: High-dose ototoxicity risk especially with aminoglycosides. Monitor K+ — hypokalemia common.

lithium.food_effect: Take with food/milk to reduce GI upset. MAINTAIN CONSISTENT SODIUM INTAKE — low-sodium diet causes lithium retention → toxicity. Dehydration causes toxicity.
lithium.notes: CRITICAL: NSAIDs, thiazides, ACE inhibitors reduce renal lithium clearance → toxicity. Dehydration/sodium restriction → lithium toxicity. Toxicity signs: tremor, ataxia, confusion, arrhythmia. No protein binding → dialysis removes lithium in overdose.
lithium.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Lithium is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to any inactive ingredient in the lithium carbonate capsule or lithium citrate products [see Adverse Reactions ( 6 )]. Known hypersensitivity to any inactive ingredient in the drug product ( 4 )
lithium.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS • Diuretics, NSAID, renin-angiotensin system antagonists, or metronidazole may increase lithium serum concentrations. Recommend frequent monitoring of serum lithium concentration and adjust dosage when necessary. ( 2.3 , 7.1 ) • Serotonergic Agents: Increased risk of serotonin syndrome when co-administered with lithium. ( 5.6 , 7.1 ) • Antipsychotics: There have been reports of
lithium.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary: Lithium may cause harm when administered to a pregnant woman. Early voluntary reports to international birth registries suggested an increase in cardiovascular malformation

vancomycin.mechanism: Inhibits cell wall synthesis by binding D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of peptidoglycan precursors
vancomycin.food_effect: IV only (systemic). Oral formulation for C. diff — not absorbed systemically.
vancomycin.notes: Red Man Syndrome: infuse over ≥60 min. Nephrotoxicity synergistic with aminoglycosides. Oral vancomycin for C. diff: NOT absorbed — safe in renal impairment. MRSA drug of choice.
vancomycin.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS Infusion Reactions Rapid bolus administration (e.g., over several minutes) may be associated with exaggerated hypotension, including shock and rarely cardiac arrest. Vancomycin hydrochloride for injection should be administered in a diluted solution over a period of not less than 60 minutes
vancomycin.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Vancomycin hydrochloride for injection is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to this antibiotic.
vancomycin.fda_drug_interactions_text: Drug Interactions Concomitant administration of vancomycin and anesthetic agents has been associated with erythema and histamine-like flushing (see Pediatric Use, PRECAUTIONS ) and anaphylactoid reactions (see ADVERSE REACTIONS ). Monitor renal function in patients receiving vancomycin and concurrent and/or sequential systemic or topical use of other potentially, neurotoxic and/or nephrotoxic drug
vancomycin.fda_pregnancy_text: Pregnancy Teratogenic Effects Animal reproduction studies have not been conducted with vancomycin. It is not known whether vancomycin can affect reproduction capacity. In a controlled clinical study,

meropenem.mechanism: Inhibits PBPs → blocks cell wall transpeptidation → bactericidal
monitoring: Seizure threshold (especially with prior CNS disease)
meropenem.notes: CRITICAL: Carbapenems reduce valproate serum level up to 90% within 24hr — do not combine if valproate essential. Extended infusion (3hr) recommended for MIC >2 mg/L organisms.
meropenem.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Meropenem for Injection, USP is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to any component of this product or to other drugs in the same class or in patients who have demonstrated anaphylactic reactions to beta (β)-lactams. Known hypersensitivity to product componen
meropenem.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Co-administration of Meropenem for Injection, USP with probenecid inhibits renal excretion of meropenem and is therefore not recommended. ( 7.1 ) The concomitant use of Meropenem for Injection, USP and valproic acid or divalproex sodium is generally not recommended. Antibacterial drugs other than carbapenems should be considered to treat infections in patients whose seizures ar
meropenem.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary There are insufficient human data to establish whether there is a drug-associated risk of major birth defects or miscarriages with meropenem in pregnant women. No fetal toxi

linezolid.mechanism: Binds 23S rRNA of 50S ribosomal subunit → inhibits initiation complex formation
linezolid.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. AVOID tyramine-rich foods (aged cheese, cured meats, wine) — MAOI-like reaction.
linezolid.notes: Weak MAO inhibitor — serotonin syndrome risk with SSRIs/SNRIs/tramadol (critical DDI). Tyramine dietary restriction required. Not intended for >28-day courses without specialist review.
linezolid.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS • Known hypersensitivity to linezolid or any of the other product components. ( 4.1 ) • Patients taking any monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOI) or within two weeks of taking an MAOI. ( 4.2 ) 4.1 Hypersensitivity Linezolid formulations are contraindicated for use in patients who h
linezolid.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Monoamine oxidase inhibitors and potential for interaction with adrenergic and serotonergic agents. ( 4.2 , 5.3 , 5.6 , 7 , 12.3 ) 7.1 Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitors Linezolid is a reversible, nonselective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase [see Contraindications ( 4.2 ) and Clinical Pharmacology ( 12.3 )]. 7.2 Adrenergic and Serotonergic Agents Linezolid has the potential for intera
linezolid.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Available data from published and postmarketing case reports with linezolid use in pregnant women have not identified a drug-associated risk of major birth defects, miscarri

insulin_aspart.mechanism: Rapid-onset insulin receptor agonism — covers post-meal glucose excursion
monitoring: Blood glucose before and 2hr after meals
insulin_aspart.food_effect: MUST be administered 0-15 minutes before or immediately after starting a meal. Dose based on carbohydrate content.
insulin_aspart.notes: Onset 10-20 min; peak 1-3hr; duration 3-5hr. High hypoglycemia risk if meal skipped after injection.
insulin_aspart.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS NOVOLOG is contraindicated: • During episodes of hypoglycemia [see Warnings and Precautions ( 5.3 )] • In patients with hypersensitivity to NOVOLOG or one of its excipients, [see Warnings and Precautions ( 5.5 )] • During episodes of hypoglycemia ( 4 ). • Hypersensitivity to NOVO
insulin_aspart.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS The table below presents clinically significant drug interactions with NOVOLOG. Drugs That May Increase the Risk of Hypoglycemia Drugs: Antidiabetic agents, ACE inhibitors, angiotensin II receptor blocking agents, disopyramide, fibrates, fluoxetine, monoamine oxidase inhibitors, pentoxifylline, pramlintide, salicylates, somatostatin analog (e.g., octreotide), and sulfonamide an
insulin_aspart.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Available information from published randomized controlled trials with insulin aspart use during the second trimester of pregnancy have not reported an association with insu

contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.mechanism: Estrogen + progestin → suppresses LH/FSH surges → inhibits ovulation; thickens cervical mucus; alters endometrium
contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Take at same time each day.
contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.notes: ENZYME INDUCERS (rifampicin, carbamazepine, phenytoin, St. John's Wort) reduce efficacy — use barrier method + higher-dose OCP. Lamotrigine clearance increased significantly by OCP — monitor seizure control.
contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Iclevia is contraindicated in females who are known to have or develop the following conditions: A high risk of arterial or venous thrombotic diseases. Examples include females who are known to: Smoke, if over age 35 [see Boxed Warning and Warnings and Precautions (5.1) ]. Have c
contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS The sections below provide information on substances for which data on drug interactions with COCs are available. There is little information available about the clinical effect of most drug interactions that may affect COCs. However, based on the known pharmacokinetic effects of these drugs, clinical strategies to minimize any potential adverse effect on contraceptive effectiv
contraceptive_pill_ethinyl_estradiol.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary There is no use for contraception in pregnancy; therefore, Iclevia should be discontinued during pregnancy. Epidemiologic studies and meta-analyses have not found an increas

testosterone.mechanism: Binds androgen receptor → promotes male secondary sexual characteristics, spermatogenesis, erythropoiesis, muscle/bone anabolism
testosterone.food_effect: Transdermal: do not apply to scrotum/damaged skin. Wash hands after. Do not shower/swim 5hr post-application.
testosterone.notes: Erythrocytosis risk — hold if Hct >54%. Stimulates PSA — monitor prostate. Increases warfarin effect. Contraindicated in prostate/breast cancer.
testosterone.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS In patients with breast cancer, androgen therapy may cause hypercalcemia by stimulating osteolysis. In this case, the drug should be discontinued. Prolonged use of high doses of androgens has been associated with the development of peliosis hepatis and hepatic neoplasms including hepatocell
testosterone.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Androgens are contraindicated in men with carcinomas of the breast or with known or suspected carcinomas of the prostate. If administered to pregnant women, androgens cause virilization of the external genitalia of the female fetus. The virilization includes clitoromegaly, abnormal

levodopa_carbidopa.mechanism: Levodopa crosses BBB → DOPA decarboxylase → dopamine. Carbidopa inhibits peripheral DOPA decarboxylase → reduces levodopa side effects, increases CNS delivery
levodopa_carbidopa.food_effect: HIGH-PROTEIN MEALS compete for absorption via large neutral amino acid transporters — take 30-60 min before or 1-2hr after protein-rich meals for maximal effect. Food reduces Cmax but protein is the key interaction.
levodopa_carbidopa.notes: MAOIs (non-selective) absolutely contraindicated — hypertensive crisis. Dopamine agonists (pramipexole) added to extend honeymoon period. Wearing-off and on-off phenomena develop over time.
levodopa_carbidopa.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Carbidopa, levodopa and entacapone tablets are contraindicated in patients: Taking nonselective monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors (e.g., phenelzine and tranylcypromine). These nonselective MAO inhibitors must be discontinued at least two weeks prior to initiating therapy with ca
levodopa_carbidopa.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Drugs metabolized by COMT: use with caution ( 5.11 , 7.2 ) Anti-hypertensive agents: dose adjustment may be required ( 7.3 ) Tricyclic antidepressants: risk of hypertension and dyskinesia reported during concomitant use with carbidopa/levodopa ( 7.4 ) Dopamine D2 receptor antagonists, isoniazid, phenytoin, papaverine and iron salts: may reduce efficacy of carbidopa, levodopa an
levodopa_carbidopa.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary There are no adequate data on the developmental risk associated with the use of carbidopa, levodopa and entacapone tablets in pregnant women. In animals, administration of c

pramipexole.mechanism: Direct D2/D3 receptor agonism in striatum and substantia nigra
pramipexole.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
pramipexole.notes: Impulse control disorders in up to 17% (gambling, shopping, hypersexuality) — counsel patients and carers. Sleep attacks without warning — no driving. Renal dose adjustment if CrCl <60.
pramipexole.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Dopamine antagonists: May diminish the effectiveness of pramipexole (7.1). 7.1 Dopamine Antagonists Since pramipexole is a dopamine agonist, it is possible that dopamine antagonists, such as the neuroleptics (phenothiazines, butyrophenones, thioxanthenes) or metoclopramide, may diminish the effectiveness of pramipexole dihydrochloride extended-release tablets.
pramipexole.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary There are no adequate data on the developmental risk associated with the use of pramipexole dihydrochloride extended-release in pregnant women. No adverse developmental effe

baclofen.mechanism: GABA-B receptor agonism → inhibits mono- and polysynaptic spinal reflexes → muscle relaxation
baclofen.food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset
baclofen.notes: NEVER stop abruptly — withdrawal can cause seizures, hyperthermia, hallucinations (especially intrathecal baclofen). Taper slowly.
baclofen.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS a. Neonatal Withdrawal Symptoms: Withdrawal symptoms have been reported starting hours to days after delivery in neonates whose mothers were treated with oral baclofen throughout pregnancy. The symptoms of withdrawal in these infants have included increased muscle tone, tremor, jitteriness,

tamsulosin.mechanism: Selective alpha-1A blockade in prostate smooth muscle → relaxes bladder neck and prostate → improved urine flow
monitoring: Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome (IFIS) — warn ophthalmologist before cataract surgery
tamsulosin.food_effect: Take 30 min after same meal each day — food reduces peak concentration and orthostatic hypotension risk.
tamsulosin.notes: Intraoperative Floppy Iris Syndrome — MUST inform surgeon before any eye surgery. Orthostatic hypotension worse with first dose. CYP3A4/2D6 inhibitors increase levels.
tamsulosin.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Tamsulosin hydrochloride capsules are contraindicated in patients known to be hypersensitive to tamsulosin hydrochloride or any component of tamsulosin hydrochloride capsules. Reactions have included skin rash, urticaria, pruritus, angioedema, and respiratory symptoms [see Advers
tamsulosin.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS • Tamsulosin hydrochloride capsules 0.4 mg should not be used with strong inhibitors of CYP3A4 (e.g., ketoconazole). Tamsulosin hydrochloride capsules should be used with caution in combination with moderate inhibitors of CYP3A4 (e.g., erythromycin), in combination with strong (e.g., paroxetine) or moderate (e.g., terbinafine) inhibitors of CYP2D6, or in patients known to be CY
tamsulosin.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Tamsulosin hydrochloride is not indicated for use in women. There are no adequate data on the developmental risk associated with the use of tamsulosin hydrochloride in pregn

finasteride.mechanism: Inhibits 5-alpha reductase type II → reduces DHT → prostate size reduction / hair follicle androgen effect reduction
monitoring: PSA (finasteride reduces PSA by 50% — adjust reference range)
finasteride.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
finasteride.notes: TERATOGENIC — pregnant women must NOT handle crushed/broken tablets. PSA is halved — a PSA that appears 'normal' may be elevated when corrected. High-grade prostate cancer concern (clinical debate).
finasteride.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Finasteride tablets USP are contraindicated in the following: • Pregnancy. Finasteride use is contraindicated in women when they are or may potentially be pregnant. Because of the ability of Type II 5α-reductase inhibitors to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to 5α-dihydrote
finasteride.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS 7.1 Cytochrome P450-Linked Drug Metabolizing Enzyme System No drug interactions of clinical importance have been identified. Finasteride does not appear to affect the cytochrome P450-linked drug-metabolizing enzyme system. Compounds that have been tested in man include antipyrine, digoxin, propranolol, theophylline, and warfarin and no clinically meaningful interactions were fo
finasteride.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Teratogenic Effects : Pregnancy Category X [ see Contraindications (4) ]. Finasteride tablets USP is contraindicated for use in women who are or may become pregnant. Finasteride tablets

acetazolamide.mechanism: Inhibits carbonic anhydrase → reduces aqueous humor formation (IOP reduction); also reduces CSF production; metabolic acidosis
acetazolamide.food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI upset. High potassium foods (bananas, oranges) recommended.
acetazolamide.notes: Sulfonamide-based — cross-reactivity with sulfa allergy possible. Hypokalemia risk. Contraindicated in hepatic cirrhosis (NH3 retention). Altitude sickness prevention/treatment.
fda_manufacturer: Heritage Pharmaceuticals Inc. d/b/a Avet Pharmaceuticals Inc.
acetazolamide.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS Fatalities have occurred, although rarely, due to severe reactions to sulfonamides including Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, fulminant hepatic necrosis, agranulocytosis, aplastic anemia, and other blood dyscrasias, and anaphylaxis. Sensitizations may recur when a sulfo
acetazolamide.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Hypersensitivity to acetazolamide or any excipients in the formulation. Since acetazolamide is a sulfonamide derivative, cross sensitivity between acetazolamide, sulfonamides and other sulfonamide derivatives is possible. Acetazolamide therapy is contraindicated in situations in wh
acetazolamide.fda_pregnancy_text: Pregnancy: Teratogenic effects Acetazolamide, administered orally or parenterally, has been shown to be teratogenic (defects of the limbs) in mice, rats, hamsters, and rabbits. There are no adequate a

enoxaparin.mechanism: Activates antithrombin III with higher anti-Xa than anti-IIa activity → inhibits factor Xa predominantly
enoxaparin.therapeutic_range: Anti-Xa 0.5-1.0 IU/mL (twice daily); 1.0-2.0 IU/mL (once daily, 4hr post-dose)
monitoring: Anti-Xa level in renal impairment/extremes of weight/pregnancy
Platelet count (HIT less common than UFH)
enoxaparin.notes: Does NOT require routine monitoring in standard-weight patients with normal renal function. Anti-Xa monitoring recommended if CrCl <30, weight <45kg or >100kg, or pregnancy. Partially reversed by protamine (not fully).
enoxaparin.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Enoxaparin sodium injection is contraindicated in patients with: • Active major bleeding • History of immune-mediated heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) within the past 100 days or in the presence of circulating antibodies [see Warnings and Precautions (5.4) ] • Known hyperse
enoxaparin.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Whenever possible, agents which may enhance the risk of hemorrhage should be discontinued prior to initiation of enoxaparin sodium therapy. These agents include medications such as: anticoagulants, platelet inhibitors including acetylsalicylic acid, salicylates, NSAIDs (including ketorolac tromethamine), dipyridamole, or sulfinpyrazone. If coadministration is essential, conduct
enoxaparin.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Placental transfer of enoxaparin was observed in the animal studies. Human data from a retrospective cohort study, which included 693 live births, suggest that enoxaparin so

clopidogrel.mechanism: Prodrug → CYP2C19 activation → active thiol metabolite irreversibly inhibits P2Y12 ADP receptor on platelets → reduced platelet aggregation
clopidogrel.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
clopidogrel.notes: CYP2C19 *2 and *3 poor metabolizers: ~30% of Asians, 2-3% of Caucasians — reduced antiplatelet effect → consider alternative (ticagrelor, prasugrel). PPIs (especially omeprazole) inhibit CYP2C19 → reduced clopidogrel activation (controversial — pantoprazole safer).
clopidogrel.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Active pathological bleeding, such as peptic ulcer or intracranial hemorrhage (4.1) Hypersensitivity to clopidogrel or any component of the product (4.2) 4.1 Active Bleeding Clopidogrel tablets are contraindicated in patients with active pathological bleeding such as peptic ulcer
clopidogrel.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS CYP2C19 inducers: Increases levels of clopidogrel active metabolite and increases platelet inhibition. ( 7.1 ) Opioids: Decreased exposure to clopidogrel. Consider use of parenteral antiplatelet agent. ( 7.3 ) Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), warfarin, selective serotonin and serotonin norepinephrine reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs, SNRIs): Increases risk of bleeding.
clopidogrel.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Available data from cases reported in published literature and postmarketing surveillance with clopidogrel use in pregnant women have not identified any drug-associated risk

aspirin.mechanism: Irreversibly acetylates COX-1 and COX-2 → reduced TXA2 (antiplatelet) and PGE2 (analgesic/anti-inflammatory)
aspirin.food_effect: Take WITH food or milk to reduce GI irritation. Enteric-coated formulation reduces direct gastric contact.
aspirin.notes: Reye's syndrome in children with viral illness — avoid under 16yr. Antiplatelet effect lasts platelet lifetime (7-10 days). Tinnitus is early sign of salicylate toxicity. Interacts with warfarin (bleeding), methotrexate (toxicity), ACE inhibitors (reduced effect).
aspirin.fda_warnings_text: Warnings Reye's syndrome : Children and teenagers who have or are recovering from chicken pox or flu-like symptoms should not use this product. When using this product, if changes in behavior with nausea and vomiting occur, consult a doctor because these symptoms could be an early sign of Reye's syn

methotrexate.mechanism: Inhibits dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) → reduces folate cofactors → inhibits DNA/RNA synthesis and purine metabolism; anti-inflammatory at low doses via adenosine pathway
methotrexate.therapeutic_range: Rheumatology: clinical response; Oncology: dose-dependent with leucovorin rescue
methotrexate.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol (hepatotoxicity).
methotrexate.notes: TERATOGEN — strict contraception required during and 6 months after. Take folic acid 5mg weekly (NOT on MTX day) to reduce side effects. NSAIDs + MTX = toxicity risk (reduce renal clearance). Trimethoprim + MTX = severe myelosuppression (additive DHFR inhibition). Weekly dosing for rheumatology — NEVER daily.
methotrexate.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Methotrexate tablets are contraindicated in: • Pregnant women receiving methotrexate tablets for treatment of non-neoplastic diseases [see Warnings and Precautions (5.1), and Use in Specific Populations (8.1, 8.3)]. • Patients with a history of severe hypersensitivity reactions,
methotrexate.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Refer to the full prescribing information for drug interactions with methotrexate. (7) 7.1 Effects of Other Drugs on Methotrexate Drugs that Increase Methotrexate Exposure Coadministration of methotrexate with the following products may increase methotrexate plasma concentrations, which may increase the risk of methotrexate severe adverse reactions. In some cases, the coadminis
methotrexate.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Methotrexate is contraindicated in pregnant women with non-neoplastic diseases [see Contraindications (4)]. Based on published reports and its mechanism of action [see Clini

budesonide.mechanism: Binds glucocorticoid receptors → reduces airway inflammation, edema, mucus secretion
budesonide.food_effect: Inhaled: rinse mouth after use to reduce oropharyngeal candidiasis. Oral capsule (Crohn's): swallow whole, do not crush.
budesonide.notes: Minimal systemic absorption vs oral steroids. CYP3A4 inhibitors (itraconazole, ritonavir) can increase systemic budesonide → adrenal suppression. Rinse mouth after inhaled use.
budesonide.fda_warnings_text: Warnings Do not use in children under 6 years of age if you have ever had an allergic reaction to any of the ingredients Ask a doctor before use if you have had recent nose ulcers or nose surgery have had a nose injury that has not healed are using a steroid medicine for asthma, allergies or skin ra

ipratropium.mechanism: Competitively inhibits M1/M3 muscarinic receptors in bronchial smooth muscle → bronchodilation; reduces secretions
monitoring: Angle-closure glaucoma if eye contact with solution
ipratropium.notes: Avoid contact with eyes (nebulizer mask) — angle-closure glaucoma risk. Additive anticholinergic effects with other antimuscarinics. Less effective for acute asthma than SABA.

donepezil.mechanism: Reversible inhibition of acetylcholinesterase → increases acetylcholine in cholinergic synapses of basal forebrain → improves cognition in Alzheimer's
donepezil.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food. If nightmares occur, switch to morning dosing.
donepezil.notes: Bradycardia/syncope — caution with sick sinus syndrome, AV block. NSAIDs risk GI bleeding with cholinesterase inhibitors. Higher dose (23mg) more GI side effects.
donepezil.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Known hypersensitivity to donepezil hydrochloride or to piperidine derivatives ( 4 ) Donepezil hydrochloride is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to donepezil hydrochloride or to piperidine derivatives.
donepezil.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS Cholinesterase inhibitors have the potential to interfere with the activity of anticholinergic medications ( 7.1 ). A synergistic effect may be expected with concomitant administration of succinylcholine, similar neuromuscular blocking agents, or cholinergic agonists ( 7.2 ). 7.1 Use with Anticholinergics Because of their mechanism of action, cholinesterase inhibitors have the
donepezil.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary There are no adequate data on the developmental risks associated with the use of donepezil hydrochloride in pregnant women. In animal studies, developmental toxicity was not

diazepam.mechanism: Positive allosteric modulator at GABA-A receptors → enhances Cl- influx → CNS depression
diazepam.therapeutic_range: Clinical anxiolysis/seizure control; avoid respiratory depression
diazepam.food_effect: Take with food to reduce GI effects. Alcohol: ABSOLUTE AVOIDANCE — additive CNS/respiratory depression.
diazepam.notes: Active metabolite desmethyldiazepam t1/2 = 36-200hr — accumulates in elderly. Withdrawal (after >2-4 weeks): taper slowly to prevent seizures. Flumazenil reversal. Avoid in elderly (Beers criteria).
diazepam.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS Risks from Concomitant Use with Opioids Concomitant use of benzodiazepiones, including diazepam tablets, and opioids may result in profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death. Because of these risks, reserve concomitant prescribing of these drugs in patients for whom alternat
diazepam.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Diazepam tablets are contraindicated in patients with a known hypersensitivity to diazepam and, because of lack of sufficient clinical experience, in pediatric patients under 6 months of age. Diazepam tablets are also contraindicated in patients with myasthenia gravis, severe respi
diazepam.fda_drug_interactions_text: Drug Interactions Opioids The concomitant use of benzodiazepines and opioids increases the risk of respiratory depression because of actions at different receptor sites in the CNS that control respiration. Benzodiazepines interact at GABA A sites and opioids interact primarily at mu receptors. When benzodiazepines and opioids are combined, the potential for benzodiazepines to significantly worsen
diazepam.fda_pregnancy_text: Neonatal Sedation and Withdrawal Syndrome Use of diazepam tablets late in pregnancy can result in sedation (respiratory depression, lethargy, hypotonia) and/or withdrawal symptoms (hyperreflexia, irri

zolpidem.mechanism: Selective GABA-A alpha-1 subunit PAM → hypnotic with less anxiolysis/muscle relaxation than BZDs
zolpidem.food_effect: Take on EMPTY STOMACH — food delays onset by 1hr and reduces Cmax. Take immediately before bed.
zolpidem.notes: Reduce dose to 5mg in women (lower clearance), elderly, hepatic impairment. Parasomnias (complex sleep behaviors) — warn patients. Not for long-term use. CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole) double levels.
zolpidem.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Zolpidem Tartrate Tablets is contraindicated in patients who have experienced complex sleep behaviors after taking Zolpidem Tartrate Tablets [see Warnings and Precautions ( 5.1 )] with known hypersensitivity to zolpidem. Observed reactions include anaphylaxis and angioedema [see
zolpidem.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS CNS depressants, including alcohol: Possible adverse additive CNS- depressant effects ( 5.1 , 7.1 ) Opioids: Concomitant use may increase risk of respiratory depression ( 5.7 , 7.1 ) Imipramine: Decreased alertness observed ( 7.1 ) Chlorpromazine: Impaired alertness and psychomotor performance observed ( 7.1 ) CYP3A4 inducers (rifampin or St. John's wort): Combination use may d
zolpidem.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Neonates born to mothers using Zolpidem late in the third trimester of pregnancy have been reported to experience symptoms of respiratory depression and sedation [see Clinic

venlafaxine.mechanism: Serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake inhibition (NE selectivity increases at higher doses)
venlafaxine.food_effect: Take with food to reduce nausea. Extended-release: swallow whole.
venlafaxine.notes: Dose-dependent hypertension (especially >300mg) — monitor BP. Severe discontinuation syndrome — taper over weeks. Serotonin syndrome with MAOIs/tramadol. CYP2D6 inhibitors increase levels.
venlafaxine.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS Clinical Worsening and Suicide Risk Patients with major depressive disorder (MDD), both adult and pediatric, may experience worsening of their depression and/or the emergence of suicidal ideation and behavior (suicidality) or unusual changes in behavior, whether or not they are taking antid
venlafaxine.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Hypersensitivity to venlafaxine hydrochloride or to any excipients in the formulation. The use of MAOIs intended to treat psychiatric disorders with venlafaxine tablets or within 7 days of stopping treatment with venlafaxine tablets is contraindicated because of an increased risk o
venlafaxine.fda_drug_interactions_text: Drug Interactions As with all drugs, the potential for interaction by a variety of mechanisms is a possibility. Alcohol A single dose of ethanol (0.5 g/kg) had no effect on the pharmacokinetics of venlafaxine or ODV when venlafaxine was administered at 150 mg/day in 15 healthy male subjects. Additionally, administration of venlafaxine in a stable regimen did not exaggerate the psychomotor and psyc
venlafaxine.fda_pregnancy_text: Clinical Worsening and Suicide Risk Patients, their families, and their caregivers should be encouraged to be alert to the emergence of anxiety, agitation, panic attacks, insomnia, irritability, hosti

bupropion.mechanism: Inhibits reuptake of norepinephrine and dopamine; no serotonergic activity
monitoring: Seizure threshold (dose-related — do not exceed 150mg per dose)
bupropion.food_effect: Take with food to reduce nausea. Extended-release: do not crush.
bupropion.notes: Lowers seizure threshold — CONTRAINDICATED in eating disorders (electrolyte imbalances), seizure history, concurrent CNS stimulants. No sexual dysfunction (unlike SSRIs). Strong CYP2D6 inhibitor — increases tamoxifen, tricyclics, many others.
bupropion.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS Bupropion hydrochloride extended-release tablets (XL) are contraindicated in patients with seizure disorder. Bupropion hydrochloride extended-release tablets (XL) are contraindicated in patients with a current or prior diagnosis of bulimia or anorexia nervosa as a higher incidenc

bupropion.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Pregnancy Exposure Registry There is a pregnancy exposure registry that monitors pregnancy outcomes in women exposed to antidepressants during pregnancy. Healthcare providers are encoura

sulfasalazine.mechanism: Cleaved by colonic bacteria → mesalazine (anti-inflammatory) + sulfapyridine (immunomodulatory, DMARD component)
sulfasalazine.food_effect: Take WITH food to reduce GI side effects. Ensure adequate hydration.
sulfasalazine.notes: G6PD deficiency → hemolytic anemia. NAT2 slow acetylators (50% of Caucasians) higher sulfapyridine levels → more side effects. Reduces folate absorption — supplement. Orange urine/contact lens staining (warn patients).
sulfasalazine.fda_warnings_text: WARNINGS Hepatic, Renal, and Hematologic Toxicity or Other Conditions Only after critical appraisal should Sulfasalazine Tablets be given to patients with hepatic or renal damage or blood dyscrasias. Deaths associated with the administration of sulfasalazine have been reported from hypersensitivity
sulfasalazine.fda_contraindications_text: CONTRAINDICATIONS Sulfasalazine Tablets are contraindicated in: Patients with intestinal or urinary obstruction, Patients with porphyria as sulfonamides have been reported to precipitate an acute attack, Patients hypersensitive to sulfasalazine, its metabolites, sulfonamides, or salicylates.

tamoxifen.mechanism: Competitive estrogen receptor antagonism in breast tissue; partial agonism in uterus/bone
tamoxifen.therapeutic_range: Clinical (endoxifen level >5.97 ng/mL associated with benefit)
tamoxifen.food_effect: Can be taken with or without food
tamoxifen.notes: CYP2D6 METABOLIZES to endoxifen (active metabolite — main efficacy driver). CYP2D6 inhibitors (fluoxetine, paroxetine, bupropion) DRASTICALLY reduce endoxifen → reduced efficacy. Avoid CYP2D6 inhibitors during tamoxifen therapy. Uterine cancer risk — any abnormal bleeding requires urgent investigation. VTE risk — thromboprophylaxis if major surgery.
tamoxifen.fda_contraindications_text: 4 CONTRAINDICATIONS SOLTAMOX is contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity (e.g., angioedema, serious skin reactions) to tamoxifen or any other SOLTAMOX ingredient [see Adverse Reactions (6.2) ] . ( 7.1 ) Warfarin: Do not use in patients taking tamoxifen for DCIS and for reduction in breast cancer incidence in women at high risk. ( 4 ) Closely monitor coagulation indices for increased anticoagulant effect when used with tamoxifen for metastatic breast cancer or as adjuvant therapy. ( 7.2 ) 7.1 A
tamoxifen.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary SOLTAMOX can cause fetal harm when administered to a pregnant woman . There are no adequate and well- controlled studies of tamoxifen in pregnant women. There are limited po

imatinib.mechanism: Competitive inhibition of ATP binding site of BCR-ABL kinase → blocks CML proliferation; also inhibits c-KIT (GIST) and PDGFR
imatinib.food_effect: Take WITH food and large glass of water to reduce GI irritation
imatinib.notes: CYP3A4 inducers (rifampicin, carbamazepine) dramatically reduce levels — avoid or increase dose. CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole) increase levels. Fluid retention common — weigh patients regularly.
imatinib.fda_drug_interactions_text: 7 DRUG INTERACTIONS CYP3A4 inducers may decrease imatinib mesylate C max and area under curve (AUC). ( 2.12 , 7.1 , 12.3 ) CYP3A4 inhibitors may increase imatinib mesylate C max and AUC. ( 7.2 , 12.3 ) Imatinib mesylate is an inhibitor of CYP3A4 and CYP2D6 which may increase the C max and AUC of other drugs. ( 7.3 , 7.4 , 12.3 ) Patients who require anticoagulation should receive low-molecular wei
imatinib.fda_pregnancy_text: 8.1 Pregnancy Risk Summary Imatinib mesylate tablets can cause fetal harm when administered to a pregnant woman based on human and animal data. There are no clinical studies regarding use of imatinib

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive anticoagulation + platelet inhibition
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination unless benefit proven (e.g. mechanical valve); use PPI for GI protection

drug_interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2C9 → warfarin metabolism reduced → INR increases
drug_interactions.management: Reduce warfarin dose 35-65%. Monitor INR twice weekly for 4 weeks.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole strongly inhibits CYP2C9 → warfarin AUC increases 2-4x
drug_interactions.management: Reduce warfarin dose ~50%. Daily INR monitoring during fluconazole course.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9 + reduces intestinal vitamin K
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR frequently; expect warfarin dose reduction.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 component of warfarin metabolism; also reduces gut vitamin K synthesis
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR 2-3 days after starting ciprofloxacin and after stopping.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 (minor warfarin pathway) + reduces gut flora vitamin K
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR closely during course.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin MBI of CYP3A4 → simvastatin exposure increases 8-10x → rhabdomyolysis
drug_interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED. Stop simvastatin during clarithromycin course. Switch to pravastatin.

drug_interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 MBI → atorvastatin AUC increases 4-5x
drug_interactions.management: Use lowest atorvastatin dose (10mg) or switch to pravastatin/rosuvastatin.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone CYP3A4 inhibition → atorvastatin AUC increases 2-3x
drug_interactions.management: Limit atorvastatin to 40mg/day. Monitor for myopathy.

drug_interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition by amiodarone + P-gp inhibition → simvastatin 6x increase
drug_interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED >20mg simvastatin with amiodarone. Switch to pravastatin.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-gp and CYP3A4 → digoxin AUC increases 50-100%
drug_interactions.management: Reduce digoxin dose 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor levels closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits P-gp → reduced digoxin renal/intestinal secretion → toxicity
drug_interactions.management: Monitor digoxin levels. Consider dose reduction.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Contrast nephropathy → AKI → metformin accumulates → lactic acidosis
drug_interactions.management: Hold metformin 24-48hr before IV contrast; resume 48hr after if Cr stable.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Furosemide may increase metformin AUC by reducing tubular secretion
drug_interactions.management: Monitor renal function. High furosemide doses may worsen dehydration → lactic acidosis risk.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Both cause K+ retention → additive hyperkalemia risk
drug_interactions.management: Monitor K+ weekly for 4 weeks, then monthly. Target K+ <5.5 mEq/L.

drug_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandins → blunt ACEi antihypertensive effect + AKI risk
drug_interactions.management: Avoid NSAIDs if possible. Monitor BP and renal function closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effect + CYP2D6 inhibition impairs tramadol metabolism → serotonin syndrome
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination. If necessary, use lowest doses and monitor for serotonin syndrome signs.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluoxetine is a strong CYP2D6 inhibitor → metoprolol levels increase 4-5x
drug_interactions.management: Reduce metoprolol dose significantly. Monitor HR and BP. Consider beta-blocker without CYP2D6 metabolism.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Calcium forms insoluble chelate with levothyroxine in GI tract → reduced absorption
drug_interactions.management: Separate by AT LEAST 4 hours (levothyroxine first, then calcium 4hr+ later).

drug_interactions.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acid → less ionization of levothyroxine → reduced absorption
drug_interactions.management: Take levothyroxine 30-60min before omeprazole. Monitor TSH after PPI initiation.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 → phenytoin levels increase → toxicity
drug_interactions.management: Monitor phenytoin levels closely. May need 30-50% dose reduction.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Phenytoin induces CYP2C9 → warfarin metabolism accelerated. Then phenytoin also displaces warfarin protein binding initially → complex biphasic interaction.
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR very closely. Interaction is unpredictable.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase → 6-MP (azathioprine metabolite) cannot be inactivated → severe myelosuppression/toxicity
drug_interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED unless dose reduced to 25% of azathioprine dose with intensive CBC monitoring.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive QT prolongation → increased torsades de pointes risk
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination in patients with cardiac risk factors. ECG monitoring if necessary.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive QT prolongation
drug_interactions.management: Monitor QTc. Avoid in patients with hypokalemia or baseline QTc >450ms.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin MBI CYP3A4 → cyclosporine levels increase 3-5x → nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity
drug_interactions.management: Monitor cyclosporine trough daily. Reduce cyclosporine dose 50-75%.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Cyclosporine inhibits OATP1B1 and P-gp → atorvastatin AUC increases 8-10x → myopathy
drug_interactions.management: Atorvastatin max 10mg/day with cyclosporine. Prefer pravastatin.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 → theophylline AUC increases 1.5-3x → seizures, arrhythmias
drug_interactions.management: Monitor theophylline levels. Reduce theophylline dose 50% or avoid.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Disulfiram-like reaction: metronidazole inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase → acetaldehyde accumulation → flushing, vomiting, hypotension
drug_interactions.management: ABSOLUTE prohibition of alcohol during metronidazole and for 48 hours after completion.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive CNS and respiratory depression — synergistic at opioid and GABA-A receptors
drug_interactions.management: AVOID combination if possible. If required, use lowest doses. Naloxone available. Monitor respiratory rate.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effects + CYP2D6 competition → serotonin syndrome risk
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination. If necessary, use lowest doses and monitor for serotonin syndrome (hyperthermia, agitation, clonus).

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP2D6 → impairs codeine → morphine conversion → reduced analgesia AND accumulates norcodeine → CNS effects
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination. Use alternative analgesic not dependent on CYP2D6 (e.g. paracetamol, NSAIDs).

drug_interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin MBI of CYP3A4 → fentanyl levels increase dramatically → respiratory depression
drug_interactions.management: Avoid or reduce fentanyl dose 50-75%. Intensive monitoring. Consider opioid rotation.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Combined neurotoxicity — irreversible brain damage reported at high doses; additive EPS and QT prolongation
drug_interactions.management: Use lowest effective doses. Monitor lithium levels and neurological status closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 → clozapine levels increase 2-3x → seizures, agranulocytosis risk
drug_interactions.management: Monitor clozapine levels closely. Reduce clozapine dose or use alternative antibiotic (avoid fluoroquinolones).

drug_interactions.mechanism: Carbamazepine is potent CYP3A4 inducer → quetiapine AUC reduced 85-90%
drug_interactions.management: 5-fold dose increase required. Prefer alternative mood stabilizer/anticonvulsant if possible.

drug_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandins → reduce renal lithium clearance → lithium toxicity
drug_interactions.management: Avoid NSAIDs with lithium if possible. Use paracetamol instead. If unavoidable, monitor lithium level closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce angiotensin II → decreased aldosterone → sodium loss → kidney compensates by retaining lithium
drug_interactions.management: Monitor lithium levels within 1 week of starting/changing ACE inhibitor dose.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Loop diuretics cause sodium depletion → kidney reabsorbs more lithium to conserve sodium → lithium toxicity
drug_interactions.management: Monitor lithium levels after starting diuretic. Maintain adequate sodium/fluid intake.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Valproate inhibits UGT1A4 → lamotrigine glucuronidation reduced → lamotrigine levels double
drug_interactions.management: When adding valproate to lamotrigine, halve lamotrigine dose. Titrate slowly.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Carbapenems inhibit VPA tubular reabsorption and increase glucuronidation → VPA levels drop 50-90% within 24hr
drug_interactions.management: AVOID combination if valproate essential. If carbapenem required, switch to alternative anticonvulsant (levetiracetam, phenobarbital). Monitor VPA levels daily.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP2C9 → warfarin metabolism accelerated → INR falls
drug_interactions.management: Increase warfarin dose (may need to double). Monitor INR weekly during carbamazepine initiation/dose changes.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 → OCP metabolism accelerated → contraceptive failure risk
drug_interactions.management: Use non-hormonal contraception or depot medroxyprogesterone. If OCP used, higher-dose pill + barrier method.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 → reduced clopidogrel activation → reduced antiplatelet effect
drug_interactions.management: Use pantoprazole (weaker CYP2C19 inhibition) instead of omeprazole/esomeprazole.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive anticoagulation: LMWH anti-Xa effect + aspirin platelet inhibition + GI mucosal damage → bleeding
drug_interactions.management: Combination used clinically (ACS) — ensure benefits outweigh bleeding risk. Add PPI for GI protection.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effect → serotonin syndrome; also CYP2D6 competition
drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination. Use non-serotonergic analgesic.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Bupropion inhibits CYP2D6 → tamoxifen active metabolite endoxifen levels reduced → reduced breast cancer efficacy
drug_interactions.management: AVOID bupropion in breast cancer patients on tamoxifen. Use alternative antidepressant (venlafaxine, mirtazapine).

drug_interactions.mechanism: Bupropion inhibits CYP2D6 → impaired codeine activation → reduced analgesia
drug_interactions.management: Use alternative analgesic not dependent on CYP2D6 activation.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Linezolid is MAO inhibitor + serotonin reuptake inhibition → additive → serotonin syndrome
drug_interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED. Washout SSRIs before linezolid. If urgent linezolid needed, stop SSRI and wait 2 weeks (or 5 for fluoxetine).

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity — both can cause tubular damage
drug_interactions.management: Monitor renal function and drug levels closely. Ensure adequate hydration.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Metoclopramide is dopamine D2 antagonist → directly antagonizes levodopa's central dopaminergic effect
drug_interactions.management: AVOID metoclopramide in Parkinson's disease. Use domperidone (does not cross BBB) for nausea.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluoxetine strongly inhibits CYP2D6 → tamoxifen → endoxifen conversion blocked → reduced breast cancer protection
drug_interactions.management: AVOID fluoxetine/paroxetine with tamoxifen. Use venlafaxine or citalopram for hot flashes/depression.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive DHFR inhibition → severe myelosuppression (pancytopenia) — both inhibit folate pathway
drug_interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED in high-dose MTX. In low-dose RA MTX: avoid TMP-SMX if alternatives exist; if unavoidable monitor CBC closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal tubular secretion of MTX → MTX accumulates → myelosuppression/mucositis
drug_interactions.management: Avoid NSAIDs, especially perioperatively. Use paracetamol. If NSAID required, hold MTX 24hr before/after and monitor closely.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 → imatinib AUC reduced 70% → loss of CML/GIST efficacy
drug_interactions.management: Avoid carbamazepine. Switch to levetiracetam or other non-inducing anticonvulsant. If unavoidable, increase imatinib dose.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Testosterone increases warfarin effect via unclear mechanism (possibly reduced protein binding + direct hepatic effects)
drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR closely when starting/stopping testosterone. Warfarin dose reduction likely needed.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 → theophylline clearance reduced 30-50%
drug_interactions.management: Reduce theophylline dose 30-50% or monitor levels closely. Use azithromycin if possible.

food_drug_interactions.effect: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin anticoagulation
food_drug_interactions.management: Maintain CONSISTENT vitamin K intake. Do not avoid — just keep intake stable.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Competitive pharmacodynamic antagonism at VKORC1

food_drug_interactions.effect: Calcium chelates levothyroxine → absorption reduced 20-40%
food_drug_interactions.management: Separate by 4+ hours. Take levothyroxine on empty stomach first.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: GI chelation complex formation

food_drug_interactions.effect: Coffee reduces levothyroxine absorption by up to 36%
food_drug_interactions.management: Take levothyroxine 30-60 min before coffee.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Unknown — possibly tannin binding or GI motility

food_drug_interactions.effect: Soy reduces levothyroxine absorption
food_drug_interactions.management: Separate by 4 hours.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Phytate binding and enterohepatic circulation interference

food_drug_interactions.effect: CYP3A4 intestinal inhibition → Cmax +35%, AUC +40%
food_drug_interactions.management: AVOID grapefruit entirely during amlodipine therapy.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Furanocoumarins in grapefruit irreversibly inhibit intestinal CYP3A4

food_drug_interactions.effect: AUC increases 1.5-2.5x with large quantities
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid >240mL (1 cup) grapefruit juice per day.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: AUC increases 3-4x even with small grapefruit amounts
food_drug_interactions.management: AVOID grapefruit entirely.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: 15mg/20mg: food INCREASES bioavailability 39% — REQUIRED for adequate exposure
food_drug_interactions.management: 15mg and 20mg doses MUST be taken with a meal. 10mg dose: with or without food.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Solubility enhancement in fed state

food_drug_interactions.effect: Food reduces GI side effects and slows absorption (Cmax -17%) without reducing AUC
food_drug_interactions.management: Take WITH meals (breakfast and dinner) to minimize GI adverse effects.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Slower gastric emptying reduces peak concentration

food_drug_interactions.effect: ANY food (or non-water drink) reduces bioavailability >90%
food_drug_interactions.management: Take with ONLY plain water (240mL), 30-60 min before first food/drink of day. Remain upright.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: GI chelation and reduced absorption in alkaline pH

food_drug_interactions.effect: Calcium/Mg++/Al+++ chelate ciprofloxacin → absorption reduced 50-90%
food_drug_interactions.management: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after dairy/antacids.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Chelation of fluoroquinolone by divalent/trivalent cations

food_drug_interactions.effect: Must be taken before meals — food reduces absorption and requires pre-meal dosing for activation
food_drug_interactions.management: Take 30-60 min BEFORE breakfast (or largest meal).
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Acid activation requires parietal cell activation by meal

food_drug_interactions.effect: Food slows absorption → reduces orthostatic hypotension risk
food_drug_interactions.management: Take WITH food to reduce postural hypotension.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Slower Cmax with food reduces initial vasodilation

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit has minimal clinically significant effect on warfarin
food_drug_interactions.management: No restriction needed for warfarin.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Warfarin primarily CYP2C9 — not significantly CYP3A4

food_drug_interactions.effect: Caffeine and theophylline are both methylxanthines — additive toxicity
food_drug_interactions.management: Limit caffeine intake. Monitor for tachycardia, tremor, nausea.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive PDE inhibition and adenosine antagonism

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit increases alprazolam AUC ~2x → excess sedation
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid grapefruit.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit increases fentanyl exposure significantly → respiratory depression risk
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid grapefruit during fentanyl therapy.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit may increase quetiapine exposure via CYP3A4 inhibition
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid large quantities of grapefruit.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit increases carbamazepine levels → toxicity (diplopia, ataxia)
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid grapefruit during carbamazepine therapy.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: Protein-rich foods compete with levodopa for transport across GI and BBB via LNAA transporter
food_drug_interactions.management: Take levodopa 30-60 min before meals or 1-2hr after. Low-protein diet during daytime (protein redistribution diet) helps.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Competition for large neutral amino acid transporter (LNAA) in gut and BBB

food_drug_interactions.effect: Sodium depletion → kidney increases lithium reabsorption → lithium toxicity
food_drug_interactions.management: Maintain normal consistent sodium intake. Increase fluid intake in hot weather. Avoid crash diets.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Renal sodium-lithium competition: proximal tubule reabsorbs lithium when sodium depleted

food_drug_interactions.effect: Acute: CYP2C9 inhibition → warfarin increase. Chronic: CYP2C9 induction → warfarin decrease. Variable.
food_drug_interactions.management: Maintain consistent (low) alcohol intake. Abstinence preferred. Monitor INR closely.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Acute: competitive CYP2C9 inhibition. Chronic: CYP2C9 induction

food_drug_interactions.effect: Additive hepatotoxicity — both cause liver damage; increased risk of fibrosis
food_drug_interactions.management: ABSOLUTE alcohol avoidance during methotrexate therapy.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive hepatocellular toxicity

food_drug_interactions.effect: MAO inhibition by linezolid + dietary tyramine → sympathomimetic crisis (hypertensive emergency, headache, palpitations)
food_drug_interactions.management: Strict tyramine dietary restriction during linezolid therapy. Each meal <10mg tyramine.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: MAO-A inhibition → tyramine not metabolized in gut/liver → pressor effect

food_drug_interactions.effect: Grapefruit increases sildenafil AUC up to 2x → hypotension, flushing, visual disturbance
food_drug_interactions.management: Avoid grapefruit.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

food_drug_interactions.effect: Calcium, tannins, polyphenols chelate iron → absorption reduced 30-60%
food_drug_interactions.management: Take iron on empty stomach with water or vitamin C (enhances absorption). Separate from calcium/tea/coffee by 2 hours.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Chelation and tannin binding prevent iron absorption

food_drug_interactions.effect: Calcium/Mg++/Al+++ chelate doxycycline → absorption reduced (less than other tetracyclines — can be taken with food)
food_drug_interactions.management: Can take with food but separate from dairy/antacids by 2 hours.
food_drug_interactions.mechanism: Divalent cation chelation (less severe than other tetracyclines due to higher protein binding)
